title: Local AI Agents Masterclass (Gemma 4 + OpenCode + Odysseus tutorial)
summary: Jordan Urbs demonstrates running two types of local AI agents: Odysseus (workspace agent) and OpenCode (build agent), both executed on personal hardware. The tutorial covers installing Odysseus via Docker, running Gemma 4 locally through Ollama at 7.4GB RAM, and using Claude Opus as a fallback teacher model for self-healing agent skills.
writeup: The tutorial walks through running two local AI agents on personal hardware: Odysseus, a workspace agent, and OpenCode, a build agent. It covers installing Odysseus via Docker, serving Gemma 4 locally through Ollama at 7.4GB RAM, and configuring Claude Opus as a fallback teacher model for self-healing agent skills. The setup matters because it keeps agent execution on local hardware while reserving a stronger remote model for cases the local model cannot handle. Key takeaways: local inference is viable at modest memory budgets, and a teacher-model fallback offers a path to recover and improve agent skills.
